Description.Name: Blind Spot
Description.Details: Several days have passed since Liberation Day. The three Ghost Bear civilians are gone, transferred to Joanna's custody along with the two rescued Tribe operatives who kept talking about purges and blood debts. The crew hasn't spoken much about any of it since the transfer, but the atmosphere aboard the Argo remains tense and uncertain. Everyone's working, performing their duties with professional efficiency, but the easy camaraderie that used to fill the corridors has been replaced by something more strained. The HPG relay activates with an incoming transmission using Tribe encryption protocols. Joanna McEvedy appears on the viewscreen, her expression focused and professional, all business today with no trace of the zealotry that colored her previous communications. Her tone is tactical, efficient, matter-of-fact. "Commander [[COMMANDER.LastName,{COMMANDER.LastName}]]. We've identified a high-value target that requires immediate action. The [[DM.Factions[faction_GhostBearWatch],Ghost Bear Watch]] maintains a forward intelligence post in a remote region of St. Andreas that coordinates surveillance of [[DM.Factions[faction_MinnesotaTribe],Tribe]] movements across the entire Periphery sector. This installation has been tracking our cell locations, monitoring our communications, and feeding precise targeting data to Watch strike teams for months." She transmits tactical data to the main display, showing facility schematics and regional maps. "Our intelligence network has identified a narrow operational window where the garrison will be at reduced strength due to a scheduled rotation cycle. Your objective is to capture the facility intact and extract all intelligence data from their computer systems before Watch command can initiate remote purge protocols. We need to know the full scope of their surveillance operations against our people. The data inside those servers could reveal Watch operational plans against dozens of our cells across the sector and potentially save hundreds of lives." "Expected opposition includes standard garrison forces protecting the installation, nothing beyond typical military resistance. The location is remote and isolated from major population centers, which means minimal risk of third-party interference or civilian complications during the operation." She pauses, meeting the camera directly with an expression of professional concern. "This intelligence could save lives, Commander. Our people are being hunted systematically because Watch intelligence knows where they are, who they talk to, how they move. This data tells us exactly how they know all of that. It's a defensive operation, not an offensive strike. We need this information to protect our cells from future raids." The transmission ends, leaving silence filling the briefing room as the crew processes what they just heard. Darius pulls up the mission data on his console and reviews it methodically. "This looks like a standard base capture on paper. Military intelligence post, isolated location, and with clear objectives. It's really textbook stuff." He looks up from the display with visible caution in his expression. "But after what we've learned in recent operations, I want independent verification before we commit. Farah, can you cross-reference the facility coordinates against known Watch deployment records and civilian databases during transit? I want to confirm this installation is actually what Joanna says it is." Farah nods slowly, her expression thoughtful and careful. "I can access civilian databases and compare satellite imagery if we have it available. See if the installation profile matches what Joanna's describing in her briefing. It'll take some time to compile everything, but we should have solid answers before we arrive." "The operation itself sounds legitimate from a military standpoint. Capturing enemy intelligence installations is standard warfare doctrine. But given recent discrepancies, I want to verify what we can independently." Yang leans against the wall with his arms crossed, his skepticism plain on his face. "Mission makes sense on paper, I'll give her that. Intelligence post, surveillance data, defensive objective to protect your own people from enemy strikes. That's all textbook legitimate military operations." "But I don't trust anything Joanna sends us anymore without checking it twice, maybe three times. We verify everything before we pull triggers from now on." Sumire brings up navigation data and regional analysis on her console. "Remote location confirmed by independent stellar cartography. Population density in the target region is extremely low, with the nearest civilian settlement located approximately eighty kilometers from the facility coordinates." "The isolation factor works in our favor from a tactical perspective. Limited reinforcement potential for the garrison means we can control the engagement envelope more effectively." Darius reviews the mission parameters one final time before looking up at you. "Boss, this looks clean from every angle I can see. Military target, clear objective, isolated location with minimal civilian risk. Nothing in the briefing suggests questionable targeting or the kind of intelligence problems we've encountered before." "But we verify everything during transit anyway. Farah checks the databases for inconsistencies. I cross-reference Watch deployment patterns against historical records. Sumire analyzes whatever satellite data we can access. We go into this operation with full information this time, not blind trust." The crew exchanges glances around the briefing room, and the tension is palpable despite the apparently legitimate mission parameters. No one is enthusiastic about working for Joanna anymore. No one is eager to accept her intelligence at face value. But no one can identify an obvious problem with this particular operation either. The target appears to be exactly what she claims it is.

Description.Name: Prepare for deployment
Description.Details: Darius logs the mission acceptance once you nod the go ahead. "Understood, Commander. Plotting the approach vector to the Leopard." Yang heads for the mech bay. "I'll start prepping for assault-class opposition. We're going to need heavy firepower to crack that facility." The Argo begins preparations for the short trip to the mission's location. [A new contract is now available in the Command Center]
